name: Snowdrop Kitten
projectTitle: Indoor PM2.5 Exposure Estimator: Candles & Cooking
description: This tool estimates indoor PM2.5 concentration and inhaled dose for two common household emission sources — candle combustion and gas stovetop cooking — across multiple ventilation scenarios. It requires user inputs including room dimensions, number of sources, emission rate, time spent in the room, breathing rate, and background PM2.5 concentration; ventilation scenarios (such as window closed, window open, or range hood on) are toggled interactively. The tool applies a steady-state mass balance model to compute concentration, then multiplies by breathing rate and time to estimate inhaled dose in micrograms. Outputs should be interpreted as order-of-magnitude estimates useful for comparing scenarios and identifying which ventilation conditions meaningfully reduce exposure and are displayed alongside the EPA 24-hour PM2.5 standard of 35 μg/m³ as a reference benchmark.
